chief_complaint: Patients presented with septic cardiomyopathy, some without chest pain, mimicking acute coronary syndrome.
history: Four cases of septic cardiomyopathy are presented, highlighting ECG and troponin elevations without significant coronary stenosis, likely due to vascular involvement and cellular damage from sepsis.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided.
labs: ECG abnormalities and troponin elevations are noted, without significant stenosis on coronary angiography.
